cast.role: Dr. Maeve Callahan — Senior ER attending, 40s, carries the night shift on her back and a secret drinking problem in her locker
Nurse Theo Okafor — Charge nurse, 30s, the emotional spine of the unit, hiding a whistleblower complaint he hasn't filed yet
Dr. Reza Shirazi — Second-year resident, brilliant and brittle, on academic probation after a near-miss
Sister Agnes — Retired nun turned night-shift chaplain, 70s, knows every secret in the building
Paramedic Dana Voss — Freelance contractor, brings in the complicated cases and complicated history with Maeve
Administrator Clive Harte — Night-shift operations manager, cutting beds to hit quarterly targets

locations.description: St. Brendan's Emergency Room — fluorescent-lit chaos, curtained bays, the permanent smell of antiseptic and anxiety
Staff break room behind the nurses' station — burnt coffee, a dying microwave, the real decisions get made here
Ground-floor chapel, rarely used for prayer, often used for conversations no one wants overheard
Exterior loading dock — cold air, cigarette breaks, the threshold between outside world and hospital reality
Third-floor corridor outside Harte's office — where policies that kill people are written on whiteboards

hard_facts: Maeve has been sober for four years before the series begins
Her ex-husband filed the original complaint that ended her hospital privileges in Cork
Dana knows and has been enabling her silence
threads.label: Maeve's Relapse Arc
threads.resolution: Maeve's drinking escalates through the season until a critical decision under impairment forces her to choose between covering it up and coming clean — she chooses truth, enters a monitored programme, loses her attending status temporarily

hard_facts: The complaint names Harte by name
Theo's immigration status makes retaliation existentially threatening to him
Sister Agnes is the only person he has told
threads.label: Theo's Whistleblower Dilemma
threads.resolution: Theo's complaint about Harte's bed-cuts causing a preventable death sits unsigned for six episodes; a second death forces him to file it, triggering an investigation that reshapes the hospital's power structure

hard_facts: The original near-miss involved a falsified chart, not Reza's clinical error
The attending who falsified it is now on the hospital board
Maeve suspects but has never confronted him
threads.label: Reza's Second Chance
threads.resolution: Reza's probation is secretly tied to a cover-up from his intern year; across the season he reconstructs what really happened and accepts responsibility publicly, clearing his record honestly rather than politically

hard_facts: The acquisition target is a private US chain
Harte genuinely believes fewer beds means faster throughput — he has convinced himself it is clinical, not financial
Three patients have been harmed by the policy before the season begins
threads.label: Harte's Bed-Cut Conspiracy
threads.resolution: Harte is executing a board directive to make the ER financially unattractive ahead of a private-equity acquisition; when this becomes public via Theo's complaint, the acquisition collapses and Harte resigns

hard_facts: Dana left Cork without explanation — Maeve never knew the real reason
Dana's contractor status is her deliberate choice: she refuses to be institutionally trapped again
Their first scene together in the ER is a reunion neither acknowledges aloud
threads.label: Maeve–Dana History
threads.resolution: It emerges that Maeve and Dana were together during Maeve's Cork posting; Dana transferred to St. Brendan's partially to keep an eye on her; by the finale they acknowledge the relationship ended because Maeve chose the bottle over Dana, and begin cautiously rebuilding
